Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis

Source: vignettes/longitudinal-archives.Rmd
longitudinal-archives.Rmd
library(onet2r)
library(tibble)

Read vignette("longitudinal-onet-background", package = "onet2r") before interpreting changes. This article focuses on the mechanics: assemble a panel, bridge taxonomy vintages, reconcile adjacent releases, and summarize the result.

Start with the Release Table

Live release discovery reads the public O*NET Resource Center archive page. The package function returns one row per downloadable text archive. The example below uses a small representative table so the article builds without network access.

releases <- tibble(
  version = c("30.3", "30.2", "30.1"),
  release_date = as.Date(c("2026-05-01", "2026-02-01", "2025-11-01")),
  year = c(2026L, 2026L, 2025L),
  month = c("May", "February", "November"),
  soc_vintage = c("2019", "2019", "2019"),
  text_url = c(
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_3_text.zip",
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_2_text.zip",
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_1_text.zip"
  ),
  dictionary_url = c(
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.3/excel/",
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.2/excel/",
    "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.1/excel/"
  )
)

head(releases, 3)
#> # A tibble: 3 × 7
#>   version release_date  year month    soc_vintage text_url        dictionary_url
#>   <chr>   <date>       <int> <chr>    <chr>       <chr>           <chr>         
#> 1 30.3    2026-05-01    2026 May      2019        https://www.on… https://www.o…
#> 2 30.2    2026-02-01    2026 February 2019        https://www.on… https://www.o…
#> 3 30.1    2025-11-01    2025 November 2019        https://www.on… https://www.o…

In an interactive workflow, call onet_releases() and then choose the versions you want to compare.

Assemble a Descriptor Panel

onet_archive_read() normalizes descriptor-like archive tables into a long schema. onet_panel() row-binds the same table across releases. This example uses a tiny panel with the same columns.

panel <- tibble(
  release_version = rep(c("1.0", "2.0"), each = 4),
  release_date = rep(as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01")), each = 4),
  soc_vintage = "2019",
  domain = "Abilities",
  onet_soc_code = rep(
    c("11-1011.00", "15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "41-1011.00"),
    2
  ),
  soc_code = rep(c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"), 2),
  title = "Occupation",
  element_id = "1.A.1.a.1",
  element_name = "Oral Comprehension",
  scale_id = "IM",
  data_value = c(4, 4, 4, 4, 4, 5, 4, 5),
  source_date = c(
    as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01")),
    as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2020-01-01"))
  ),
  domain_source = "Analyst"
)

head(panel, 6)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 13
#>   release_version release_date soc_vintage domain   onet_soc_code soc_code title
#>   <chr>           <date>       <chr>       <chr>    <chr>         <chr>    <chr>
#> 1 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Occu…
#> 2 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Occu…
#> 3 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 29-1141.00    29-1141  Occu…
#> 4 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 41-1011.00    41-1011  Occu…
#> 5 2.0             2021-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Occu…
#> 6 2.0             2021-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Occu…
#> # ℹ 6 more variables: element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, scale_id <chr>,
#> #   data_value <dbl>, source_date <date>, domain_source <chr>

For live data, use the same pattern with archive helpers: onet_panel("Abilities", versions = c("30.2", "30.3"), scale = "IM").

Bridge Taxonomy Vintages

When both releases use the same SOC vintage, the bridge is one-to-one. Across vintages, onet_crosswalk_bridge() composes official adjacent crosswalks and labels split or merge rows.

bridge <- tibble(
  from_vintage = "2019",
  to_vintage = "2019",
  from_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),
  to_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),
  from_title = "Occupation",
  to_title = "Occupation",
  step_count = 0L,
  map_type = "one_to_one",
  crosswalk_weight = 1
)

head(bridge, 6)
#> # A tibble: 4 × 9
#>   from_vintage to_vintage from_soc_code to_soc_code from_title to_title  
#>   <chr>        <chr>      <chr>         <chr>       <chr>      <chr>     
#> 1 2019         2019       11-1011       11-1011     Occupation Occupation
#> 2 2019         2019       15-1252       15-1252     Occupation Occupation
#> 3 2019         2019       29-1141       29-1141     Occupation Occupation
#> 4 2019         2019       41-1011       41-1011     Occupation Occupation
#> # ℹ 3 more variables: step_count <int>, map_type <chr>, crosswalk_weight <dbl>

Reconcile Adjacent Releases

onet_panel_reconcile() compares adjacent releases and classifies each matched occupation-element-scale pair.

changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge)

head(changes[, c(
  "from_soc_code",
  "to_soc_code",
  "from_value",
  "to_value",
  "value_change",
  "date_changed",
  "change_type",
  "safely_comparable"
)], 8)
#> # A tibble: 4 × 8
#>   from_soc_code to_soc_code from_value to_value value_change date_changed
#>   <chr>         <chr>            <dbl>    <dbl>        <dbl> <lgl>       
#> 1 11-1011       11-1011              4        4            0 FALSE       
#> 2 15-1252       15-1252              4        5            1 TRUE        
#> 3 29-1141       29-1141              4        4            0 TRUE        
#> 4 41-1011       41-1011              4        5            1 FALSE       
#> # ℹ 2 more variables: change_type <fct>, safely_comparable <lgl>

The change classification is:

Value Changed Source Date Changed Change Type Initially Comparable
no no stale_carryforward yes
yes yes real_update yes
no yes resampled_stable yes
yes no recode_or_recalc_flag no

method_break and crosswalk_uncertain are overrides. A row is safely comparable only when the truth-table result is comparable and neither override is present.

Summarize Changes

Summaries give a quick audit of how much of the panel is safely comparable and how often each change pattern appears.

summary <- onet_change_summary(changes, by = "job_family")
head(summary, 10)
#> # A tibble: 5 × 10
#>   summary_level job_family change_type     n_pairs share_pairs mean_value_change
#>   <chr>         <chr>      <chr>             <int>       <dbl>             <dbl>
#> 1 overall       NA         stale_carryfor…       4        1                  0.5
#> 2 job_family    11         stale_carryfor…       1        0.25               0  
#> 3 job_family    15         real_update           1        0.25               1  
#> 4 job_family    29         resampled_stab…       1        0.25               0  
#> 5 job_family    41         recode_or_reca…       1        0.25               1  
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: median_abs_value_change <dbl>,
#> #   share_safely_comparable <dbl>, share_method_break <dbl>,
#> #   share_crosswalk_uncertain <dbl>

Recommended Workflow

  1. Pick a domain table such as Abilities, Skills, or Work Activities.
  2. Use onet_releases() to identify archive versions and SOC vintages.
  3. Build a panel with onet_panel().
  4. Build a bridge with onet_crosswalk_bridge().
  5. Reconcile with onet_panel_reconcile().
  6. Inspect method_break, crosswalk_uncertain, and safely_comparable before drawing substantive conclusions.

On this page

Developed by Alex Farach.
